title: Player Eligibility Announced for World Junior Championships 2026
indian_angle: This circular outlines the eligibility criteria for Indian players aiming to represent the country at the upcoming World Junior Championships in Cairo.
summary: The Badminton Association of India (BAI) has released a circular detailing the eligibility requirements for players seeking selection for the BWF World Junior Championships 2026, scheduled to be held in Cairo, Egypt.

title: Delhi World Championships 2026 Needs Gen Z, Alpha Viewers
indian_angle: With the BWF World Championships 2026 set to be hosted in Delhi, there's a focus on attracting younger Indian audiences to ensure the sport's future popularity.
summary: As Delhi prepares to host the BWF World Championships in 2026, discussions are underway on strategies to engage younger generations, Gen Z and Gen Alpha, to broaden badminton's appeal and viewership in India.

title: BAI President Sarma Addresses Monkey Incident at India Open
indian_angle: BAI President Himanta Biswa Sarma commented on the unusual incident of monkeys entering the venue during the India Open, sparking a lighthearted discussion.
summary: BAI President Himanta Biswa Sarma made a notable remark regarding an incident where monkeys were observed at the India Open venue, questioning the issue if they came to watch a game.

title: New 3x15 Scoring System to Debut in Domestic Tournaments
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India is implementing a new 3x15 scoring system across all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships for junior categories, starting in Ernakulam.
summary: Badminton India will adopt the 3x15 scoring system for all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships in the U-13, U-15, and U-17 age groups, with its debut scheduled for a tournament in Ernakulam.

title: Kidambi Srikanth Finishes Runner-Up at US Open
indian_angle: Indian shuttler Kidambi Srikanth reached the final of the US Open, showcasing strong form before faltering at the last hurdle.
summary: Indian badminton star Kidambi Srikanth put up a commendable performance at the US Open, reaching the final before being defeated by Su Li Yang.

title: Rounak Chouhan Upsets World No 6 Chou Tien Chen at US Open
indian_angle: Young Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan created a major upset by defeating World No. 6 Chou Tien Chen at the US Open, highlighting his rising potential.
summary: Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan, a self-proclaimed Cristiano Ronaldo fan, made headlines at the US Open Super 300 by stunning World No. 6 Chou Tien Chen in a notable victory.
